title: crassula cymbiformis
intro: Crassula cymbiformis, also known as the Pagasteen, is a succulent plant species native to Southern Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Crassula cymbiformis plant are often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
